critique.summary: Function '__' has 1 high-severity issue(s) (accessibility) that compromise security or correctness.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in '__' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: No direct database queries in '__' (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json) — SQL safety dimension acceptable
sql_safety.fix: If database access is added later, always use $wpdb->prepare() with proper placeholders

security.issue: '__' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json)
security.fix: Add nonce verification, capability checks, and output escaping as defensive measures even in low-risk functions

performance.issue: '__' performs potentially expensive operations in a loop without caching (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json)
performance.fix: Cache expensive computed values using wp_cache_set()/wp_cache_get() with appropriate cache group and expiry

wp_api_usage.issue: '__' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: '__' code quality is acceptable but could improve single responsibility adherence (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json, 166 lines)
code_quality.fix: Extract complex logic into well-named helper functions, each with a single clear responsibility

dependency_integrity.issue: '__' instantiates RecordManagerReadForm, UserCapabilities, FormTranslation — ensure these dependencies are available in all execution contexts (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Inject dependencies via constructor or factory pattern rather than instantiating inside the function; improves testability

i18n.issue: '__' i18n handling is acceptable (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json) — verify text domain consistency across the plugin
i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: '__' renders form inputs without associated <label> elements — screen readers cannot identify form fields, failing WCAG 2.1 success criterion 1.3.1
accessibility.fix: Add <label for='field_id'>Field Name</label> before each <input id='field_id'>, or use aria-label attribute: <input aria-label='Field Name'>

critique.key_observation: '__' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: onoffice-for-wp-websites.json)

critique.summary: Function 'track_created_date_change' has 1 critical issue(s) (security) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in 'track_created_date_change' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: No direct database queries in 'track_created_date_change' (source: woocommerce.json) — SQL safety dimension acceptable
sql_safety.fix: If database access is added later, always use $wpdb->prepare() with proper placeholders

security.issue: 'track_created_date_change' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests
security.fix: Add wp_verify_nonce($_POST['_wpnonce'], 'action_name') before processing any user input; use wp_nonce_field() in forms

performance.issue: 'track_created_date_change' has no obvious performance issues for its current scope (source: woocommerce.json)
performance.fix: Consider adding transient caching if the function's output becomes expensive as data volume grows

wp_api_usage.issue: 'track_created_date_change' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: woocommerce.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: 'track_created_date_change' (27 lines, source: woocommerce.json) lacks explicit error handling — silent failures make debugging and monitoring difficult
code_quality.fix: Add WP_Error returns for failure conditions: if (empty($result)) { return new WP_Error('no_result', __('Operation failed', 'plugin-slug')); }

dependency_integrity.issue: 'track_created_date_change' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: woocommerce.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.issue: 'track_created_date_change' may produce user-visible output without translation wrappers (source: woocommerce.json)
i18n.fix: Audit for hardcoded strings and wrap in __() or esc_html__() with the plugin text domain

accessibility.issue: 'track_created_date_change' does not produce HTML output — accessibility dimension not applicable (source: woocommerce.json)
accessibility.fix: No changes needed for accessibility in this non-rendering function

critique.key_observation: 'track_created_date_change' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests

wpcs_compliance.fix: Add /** @param, @return, @since tags above function 'parse'; Rename to snake_case convention

sql_safety.issue: No direct database queries in 'parse' (source: auxin-elements.json) — SQL safety dimension acceptable
sql_safety.fix: If database access is added later, always use $wpdb->prepare() with proper placeholders

security.issue: 'parse' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: auxin-elements.json)
security.fix: Add nonce verification, capability checks, and output escaping as defensive measures even in low-risk functions

performance.issue: 'parse' performs potentially expensive operations in a loop without caching (source: auxin-elements.json)
performance.fix: Cache expensive computed values using wp_cache_set()/wp_cache_get() with appropriate cache group and expiry

wp_api_usage.issue: 'parse' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: auxin-elements.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: 'parse' code quality is acceptable but could improve single responsibility adherence (source: auxin-elements.json, 33 lines)
code_quality.fix: Extract complex logic into well-named helper functions, each with a single clear responsibility

dependency_integrity.issue: 'parse' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: auxin-elements.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.issue: 'parse' i18n handling is acceptable (source: auxin-elements.json) — verify text domain consistency across the plugin
i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: 'parse' renders HTML (source: auxin-elements.json) — verify semantic structure uses appropriate HTML5 elements
accessibility.fix: Use semantic HTML: <nav>, <main>, <article>, <section> where appropriate; avoid div-soup for content that has semantic meaning

critique.key_observation: 'parse' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: auxin-elements.json)
